steps.title: Find the period
steps.content:
The exponent is $4x$, where $x$ is measured in years. One application of the growth factor happens when the exponent increases by $1$:
$$
4x=1
$$
$$
x=\frac{1}{4}
$$
One-fourth of a year is $3$ months.

steps.title: Interpret the factor
steps.content: The factor $1.004$ means the balance is multiplied by $$ 1.004=1+0.004=1+0.4\% $$ So every $3$ months, the balance increases by about $0.4\%$ of the balance at the beginning of that interval. The answer is B.
